audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Canaan-boundary and land-apportionment text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for south/west/north/east boundaries, Jordan and Salt Sea, nine-and-a-half-tribe allocation, Eleazar/Joshua oversight, and named tribal representatives. Strong anti-colonial and anti-modern-territorial-weaponization safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Command children of Israel and say to them: When you come into land of Canaan, this is land that shall fall to you as inheritance, land of Canaan by its borders.
Your southern side shall be from wilderness of Zin alongside Edom, and your southern border shall begin from end of Salt Sea eastward.
Your border shall turn south of ascent of Akrabbim, pass to Zin, and its outgoing shall be south of Kadesh-barnea; it shall go out to Hazar-addar and pass to Azmon.
The border shall turn from Azmon to wadi of Egypt, and its outgoing shall be at sea.
As western border, Great Sea shall be for you, and its border; this shall be your western border.
This shall be your northern border: from Great Sea you shall mark out for yourselves Mount Hor.
From Mount Hor you shall mark to entrance of Hamath, and outgoing of border shall be to Zedad.
The border shall go out to Ziphron, and its outgoing shall be Hazar-enan; this shall be your northern border.
You shall mark for yourselves eastern border from Hazar-enan to Shepham.
The border shall go down from Shepham to Riblah on east of Ain; the border shall go down and strike slope of Sea of Chinnereth eastward.
The border shall go down to Jordan, and its outgoing shall be at Salt Sea. This shall be your land by its borders around.
Moses commanded children of Israel, saying, This is land that you shall inherit by lot, which YHWH commanded to give to nine tribes and half tribe.
For tribe of sons of Reuben by houses of fathers and tribe of sons of Gad by houses of fathers have received, and half tribe of Manasseh has received, their inheritance.
The two tribes and half tribe have received their inheritance across Jordan at Jericho eastward, toward sunrise.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
These are names of men who shall cause land to be inherited by you: Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun.
One leader, one leader from each tribe you shall take to cause land to be inherited.
These are names of men: for tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
for tribe of sons of Simeon, Shemuel son of Ammihud;
for tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon;
for tribe of sons of Dan, a leader, Bukki son of Jogli;
for sons of Joseph, for tribe of sons of Manasseh, a leader, Hanniel son of Ephod;
and for tribe of sons of Ephraim, a leader, Kemuel son of Shiphtan;
for tribe of sons of Zebulun, a leader, Elizaphan son of Parnach;
for tribe of sons of Issachar, a leader, Paltiel son of Azzan;
for tribe of sons of Asher, a leader, Ahihud son of Shelomi;
and for tribe of sons of Naphtali, a leader, Pedahel son of Ammihud.
These are those YHWH commanded to cause children of Israel to inherit in land of Canaan.

translation_notes: The chapter maps an ancient idealized inheritance boundary using places whose precise identification is sometimes uncertain.
These borders are not a modern cadastral survey and must not be used as automatic contemporary state boundaries, property deeds, or permission for displacement.
The land is already inhabited within the conquest narrative; geographic precision does not erase the moral problem of prior inhabitants and dispossession.
Allocation is overseen by priestly, military/civic, and tribal representatives rather than one ruler acting alone.
The east-Jordan inheritance of Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh is acknowledged outside the Canaan boundary described here.
safety_note: Do not weaponize Numbers 34 for modern annexation, settlement expansion, ethnic cleansing, forced removal, or denial of Palestinian, Israeli, Indigenous, or any other people's human rights. Preserve the ancient boundary tradition without turning scripture into a present-day deed of dispossession.
